stance_by_source.summary: After a break above 1.18, wants to buy a dip back into the ~1.17 support shelf, targeting 1.24 then 1.31.
Tether dominance chart labelled 'confluence for BTC/XRP long' — dominance dropping into demand supports buying XRP.
Same 1.1726 long on 50x but contingent on the big coins holding their levels — it was stopped out at 1.1603 when they didn't confirm.
Older setup buying ~2.90 for 3.0+ — at a far higher price than today, now stale.
Weak tactical note flagging a break below 2.60 — stale, a different price regime entirely.
Older retest-long 2.40-2.50 for 2.85-2.95 — no longer applicable at today's ~1.19.

conflicts: The newest chart (17 Jun 2026) lays out a bullish buy into the ~1.17 support shelf, but the leveraged Telegram version of that exact trade was stopped out at 1.1603 because Bitcoin and the other big coins never confirmed — the long only works if the majors hold.
All the 2025 live-session setups (buys around 2.40-2.90, targets 3.20-3.60) are at a completely different, far higher price than today's ~1.19 and are stale — they do not apply to the current entry.
plain_english: XRP recently pushed up through $1.18, and the most recent read is bullish: the plan is to buy a dip back into the $1.17 support shelf and aim for $1.24, then $1.31. The catch is this only works if Bitcoin and the other big coins hold their own support — last time this exact buy was tried with heavy leverage it got stopped out at $1.16 because the big coins didn't hold. Older notes from 2025 talk about much higher prices ($2.40-$2.90) and no longer apply. Don't chase it here: wait for price to pull back to around $1.17 and for Bitcoin to confirm before buying, and step away if it closes below $1.16.
